content_id: (4) Meyakini tugas-tugas yang kita ketahui yang mereka laksanakan atas perintah Allah Yang Maha Tinggi, seperti bertasbih kepada Allah dan mensucikan-Nya dari segala kekurangan, dan ibadah mereka kepada-Nya siang dan malam tanpa rasa lelah atau lemah. Ada sebagian dari mereka yang memiliki tugas-tugas khusus, misalnya Jibril, yang dipercayakan untuk menyampaikan Wahyu dari Allah Yang Maha Tinggi kepada para nabi dan rasul. Mikail, yang dipercayakan dengan tugas mengurus hujan dan pertumbuhan tanaman. Israfil, yang dipercayakan untuk meniup Sangkakala (as-Sur) pada Hari Kiamat, dan ketika ciptaan akan dibangkitkan. Malaikat maut yang tugasnya mencabut nyawa pada saat kematian. Malik, yang dipercayakan untuk menjaga neraka. Para malaikat yang dipercayakan dengan janin-janin dalam kandungan, bahwa ketika anak yang belum lahir telah mencapai empat bulan dalam kandungan ibunya, maka Allah mengutus seorang malaikat kepadanya untuk menuliskan rezekinya, usianya, amal-amalnya dan apakah ia akan sengsara atau beruntung. Para malaikat yang dipercayakan untuk mencatat amal-amal setiap orang, dan ada dua malaikat untuk setiap orang - satu di sebelah kanannya dan satu di sebelah kirinya. Para malaikat yang tugasnya menanyai orang yang meninggal ketika ia diletakkan di kuburan, dua malaikat datang kepadanya dan menanyakan tentang Tuhannya, agamanya dan nabinya. Iman kepada para malaikat menghasilkan buah-buah yang sangat besar, di antaranya: (i) Pengetahuan tentang keagungan Allah Yang Maha Tinggi, Kekuasaan-Nya, otoritas-Nya dan kekuasaan-Nya. Karena keagungan makhluk ciptaan apapun menunjukkan keagungan Sang Pencipta. (ii) Rasa syukur kepada Allah Yang Maha Tinggi atas kepedulian dan perhatian-Nya terhadap kesejahteraan manusia, karena Dia mempercayakan beberapa malaikat untuk melindungi mereka dan mencatat amal-amal mereka dan tindakan-tindakan lain yang bermanfaat bagi mereka. (iii) Kecintaan terhadap para malaikat atas ibadah mereka kepada Allah Yang Maha Tinggi. Ada beberapa orang yang menyimpang yang mengingkari bahwa para malaikat adalah makhluk-makhluk fisik, dan sebaliknya mengklaim bahwa mereka hanyalah ungkapan yang merujuk kepada kekuatan kebaikan yang melekat pada makhluk-makhluk ciptaan. Perkataan ini adalah penolakan terhadap Kitab Allah Yang Maha Tinggi, Sunnah Rasul-Nya, dan ijma' kaum muslimin. Allah Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman: 'Segala puji bagi Allah Pencipta langit dan bumi, Yang menjadikan malaikat sebagai utusan-utusan (untuk mengurus berbagai macam urusan) yang mempunyai sayap, masing-masing (ada yang) dua, tiga dan empat.' [Surat Fathir (35): 1]. Dalil-dalil ini dengan jelas membuktikan bahwa para malaikat adalah makhluk-makhluk fisik dan bukan kekuatan-kekuatan abstrak, sebagaimana yang diklaim oleh orang-orang yang menyimpang, dan apa yang jelas ditunjukkan dalam teks-teks adalah sesuatu yang di atasnya terdapat ijma' kaum muslimin. Kitab-kitab-Nya: Kitab-kitab (kutub, jamak dari kitab) - artinya apa yang tertulis. Yang dimaksud di sini adalah Kitab-kitab yang Allah Yang Maha Tinggi turunkan kepada para rasul-Nya sebagai rahmat bagi ciptaan, dan sebagai petunjuk bagi mereka, untuk memungkinkan mereka mencapai keberhasilan dan kebahagiaan di dunia ini dan di Akhirat. Iman kepada Kitab-kitab mencakup empat hal: (1) Meyakini bahwa Kitab-kitab itu diturunkan oleh Allah Yang Maha Tinggi.
content_en: (4) To believe in the duties that we know of which they carry out by the command of Allaah, the Most High, such as their glorifying Allaah and declaring Him free and far removed from all imperfections, and their worshipping Him day and night without wearying or slackening. There are some of them who have particular duties, for example Jibreel, who was entrusted to convey the Revelation from Allaah, the Most High, to the prophets and messengers. Meekaa'eel, who is entrusted with the duty of looking after rainfall and the growth of plants. Israafeel, who is entrusted with blowing the Horn (as-Soor) at the Last Hour, and when the creation are to be resurrected. The angel of death whose duty it is to take the souls at the point of death. Maalik, who is entrusted to guard the Hellfire. The angels entrusted with the embryos in the womb, that when the unborn child has attained four months in its mothers womb, then Allaah sends an angel to it to write down its provision, its lifespan, its actions and whether it will be wretched or fortunate. The angels entrusted with recording the deeds of each person, and there are two angels for each person - one on his right and one on his left. The angels whose duty it is to question the deceased when he is placed in the grave, two angels come to him and ask him about his Lord, his Religion and his Prophet. Eemaan in the angels produces very great fruits, from them: (i) Knowledge of the greatness of Allaah, the Most High, His Power, His authority and His dominion. Since the greatness of any created thing shows the greatness of the Creator. (ii) Thankfulness to Allaah, the Most High, for His care and concern for the welfare of mankind, since He entrusted some angels with protecting them and recording their deeds and other actions beneficial to them. (iii) Love of the angels for their worship of Allaah, the Most High. There are some deviant folk who deny that the angels are physical beings, and claim instead that they are merely an expression referring to the power of good inherent in created beings. This saying is a denial of the Book of Allaah, the Most High, the Sunnah of His Messenger, and the consensus {ijmaa') of the Muslims. Allaah, the Most High, says, "All praise and thanks are for Allaah, the Sole Creator who brought the heavens and the earth into existence; who made the angels messengers, with His commands and prohibitions, having wings - two or three or four." [surah Faatir (35):1] "And if you could see, O Muhammad, the angels when they take out the souls of the unbelievers at death - striking their faces and their behinds." [surah al-Anfaal (8):50] "And if you could see when those who worship others along with Allaah are in the agonies of death, and the angels are spreading their hands and striking them, and saying: Deliver up your souls." [surah al-An'aam (6):93] "Until when the fear is banished from the angels* hearts they say to one another: 'What is it that your Lord has said?' They say: 'The Truth and He is the Most High, the Most Great." [surah Saba (34):23] Allaah said about the people of paradise, "And the angels enter upon them from every gate, greeting them: You are in peace and security due to your having patiently obeyed your Lord in the worldly life, so how excellent a final home is Paradise instead of the Fire." [surah ar-Ra'd (13):23-4] The Prophet said, "If Allaah loves a servant He calls to Jibreel, 'Allaah loves so and so, so love him.' So Jibreel calls out to the inhabitants of the heavens, 'Allaah loves so and so, so love him,' 50 the inhabitants of the heavens love him. Then acceptance of him is paced on the earth. " [Collected by alBukhaaree (Eng. transl. 9/430/no. 577)]. The Prophet also said, "On the day of Jumu 'ah there are angels at every door of the mosque writing down whoever comes first, and then the succeeding people in order. So when the Imaam sits, then the scrolls are rolled up and they come to hear the Reminder," [Collected by alBukhaaree (Eng. transl. 2/25/no. 51)]. These texts clearly prove that the angels are physical beings and not abstract forces, as claimed by the deviants, and what is clearly shown in the texts is something upon which there is consensus (ijmaa') of the Muslims. His Books; [128] ________________________________________________________ The Books (kutub, plural of kitaab) - meaning that which is written. What is meant here is those Books which Allaah, the Most High, sent down to His messengers as a mercy to the creation, and as guidance for them, in order to enable them to attain success and happiness in this world and in the Hereafter. Eemaan in the Books comprises four matters: (1) To believe that they were sent down by Allaah, the Most High.
content_ar: (4) To believe in the duties that we know of which they carry out by the command of Allaah, the Most High, مثل their glorifying Allaah and declaring Him free and far removed from all imperfections, and their worshipping Him day and night without wearying or slackening. There are some of them who have particular duties, على سبيل المثال Jibreel, who was entrusted to convey the الوحي from Allaah, the Most High, to the الأنبياء and الرسل. Meekaa'eel, who is entrusted with the الواجب of looking بعد rainfall and the growth of plants. Israafeel, who is entrusted with blowing the Horn (as-Soor) at the Last Hour, and عندما the creation are to be resurrected. The angel of الموت whose الواجب it is to take the النفوس at the point of الموت. Maalik, who is entrusted to guard the النار. The الملائكة entrusted with the embryos in the womb, that عندما the unborn الولد has attained four months in its mothers womb, then Allaah sends an angel to it to write down its provision, its lifespan, its actions and whether it will be wretched or fortunate. The الملائكة entrusted with recording the deeds of each person, and there are two الملائكة for each person - one on his الحق and one on his left. The الملائكة whose الواجب it is to question the deceased عندما he is placed in the القبر, two الملائكة come to him and ask him about his Lord, his Religion and his النبي. Eemaan in the الملائكة produces very great fruits, from them: (i) العلم of the greatness of Allaah, the Most High, His Power, His authority and His dominion. بما أن the greatness of any created thing shows the greatness of the Creator. (ii) Thankfulness to Allaah, the Most High, for His care and concern for the welfare of mankind, بما أن He entrusted some الملائكة with protecting them and recording their deeds and other actions beneficial to them. (iii) المحبة of the الملائكة for their worship of Allaah, the Most High. There are some deviant folk who deny that the الملائكة are physical beings, and claim instead that they are merely an expression referring to the power of الخير inherent in created beings. This saying is a denial of the الكتاب of Allaah, the Most High, the السنة of His Messenger, and the consensus {ijmaa') of the المسلمون. Allaah, the Most High, says, "All praise and thanks are for Allaah, the Sole Creator who brought the heavens and the earth into existence; who made the الملائكة الرسل, with His commands and prohibitions, having wings - two or three or four." [السورة Faatir (35):1] "And إذا you could see, O محمد ﷺ, the الملائكة عندما they take out the النفوس of the unbelievers at الموت - striking their faces and their behinds." [السورة al-Anfaal (8):50] "And إذا you could see عندما those who worship others along with Allaah are in the agonies of الموت, and the الملائكة are spreading their hands and striking them, and saying: Deliver up your النفوس." [السورة al-An'aam (6):93] "حتى عندما the الخوف is banished from the الملائكة* القلوب they say to بعضهم بعضاً: 'What is it that your Lord has said?' They say: 'The الحق
